pr.title: fix(vertex): throw clear error when document blocks use URL sources
pr.body:
Fixes #1038

When Vertex AI rejects a document block with a URL source, the error it
returns references `image.source.base64.data`, a field that has nothing
to do with document blocks. This is confusing for users debugging.

This adds a pre-flight check in `buildRequest()` that catches URL sources
before the request goes out and throws a clear `AnthropicError` with the
base64 workaround in the message. It also covers the nested case: a document
URL source inside `tool_result.content[]` hits the same misleading error
path, so anyone who fixed the top-level issue would run straight into it next.

What changed:
- `packages/vertex-sdk/src/client.ts`: `validateNoDocumentUrlSources()` called
  in the `MODEL_ENDPOINTS` block, with a `checkBlock()` helper that handles
  both the top-level and nested cases.
- `packages/vertex-sdk/tests/client.test.ts`: Four new tests covering URL
  source at top level (throws), URL source inside a tool result (throws),
  and base64/text sources (pass).
pr.author: blut-agent
